div.p: realloc() expands bitmap capacity; new words are zero-initialized; mapping logic is invariant.
The block_id acts as a logical index that remains stable across reallocations.
Safe Expansion: Because the bitmap expands linearly, logical indices 0..99 still point to map[0] and map[1] even if the physical base pointer of map changes during realloc().
When realloc() moves the block to a new memory address, all internal pointers to the bitmap must be updated.
However, User Handles (indices) are never invalidated because they represent offset-based identity, not pointer-based identity.
